title: Social demo targets human re-onboarding frustration
summary: The Kage social demo should use doodle characters to show the human repeating repo-context bedtime stories to fresh AI agents until Kage makes the repo remember and the agent starts working on its own.
body:
The Kage social demo should be a doodle-character meme, not a graph-heavy product explainer or photoreal trailer. The story is: every time the human tries to ship work, a tiny clueless agent asks a dumb repo-context question; the human repeats the same 'once upon a time in the repo' storybook; Kage stamps runbooks, decisions, bug fixes, paths, tests, and why into repo memory; the next agent recalls context and starts working without asking stupid questions; the human realizes the child has grown.
Evidence: Implemented in scripts/render-social-demo.mjs with rendered assets docs/assets/kage-social-demo.mp4 and docs/assets/kage-social-demo-poster.png, plus script notes in docs/SOCIAL_DEMO_SCRIPT.md.
Verified by: node --check scripts/render-social-demo.mjs; node scripts/render-social-demo.mjs rendered a 1600x900, 65.00s MP4
